statement.markdown:
Given a non-empty array of integers `arr` of length `n`, you are tasked to find the length of the longest increasing subsequence (LIS) in `arr`. A subsequence of `arr` is an array that can be obtained by deleting some elements from `arr` without changing the order of the remaining elements, and its length is the number of elements in the subsequence.

Note that a subsequence is not necessarily contiguous.

The solution function should return the length of the LIS.

Example:

Input: arr = [10, 22, 9, 33, 21, 50, 41, 60, 80]
Output: 6
Explanation: The longest increasing subsequence is [10, 22, 33, 50, 60, 80], it has length 6.

HINT: The dynamic programming approach is more efficient than brute force.
statement.outputFormat: The length of the longest increasing subsequence.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(n^2), where n is the length of the input array.

hints.text: Review the dynamic programming approach for finding the longest increasing subsequence.

editorial.title: Optimal Solution
editorial.intuition: The optimal solution involves maintaining a dynamic programming array `dp` of size `n`, where `dp[i]` represents the length of the LIS ending at index `i` in the input array.
editorial.optimalSolution: Maintain a dynamic programming array `dp` of size `n`, where `dp[i]` represents the length of the LIS ending at index `i` in the input array. Initialize `dp[i] = 1` for all `i`, since a single element is always an increasing subsequence of length 1. Iterate through the input array, and for each element at index `i`, update `dp[i]` as follows: if the current element is greater than the previous element, then `dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + 1`. Finally, return the maximum value in the `dp` array, which represents the length of the LIS.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution is correct because it ensures that `dp[i]` represents the length of the LIS ending at index `i` in the input array, and the maximum value in the `dp` array gives the length of the LIS.
editorial.dryRun: Example dry run with input array `[10, 22, 9, 33, 21, 50, 41, 60, 80]`.

starterCode.java: public class Solution {public int lengthOfLIS(int[] arr);}
starterCode.python: class Solution: def lengthOfLIS(self, arr: list[int]) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int lengthOfLIS(vector<int> &arr) { int n = arr.size(); int dp[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { dp[i] = 1; } for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) { if (arr[i] > arr[j]) { dp[i] = dp[i] + 1; } } } int max = 0; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (dp[i] > max) { max = dp[i]; } } return max; }};
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int lengthOfLIS(int[] arr) { int n = arr.length; int[] dp = new int[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { dp[i] = 1; } for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) { if (arr[i] > arr[j]) { dp[i] = dp[i] + 1; } } } int max = 0; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (dp[i] > max) { max = dp[i]; } } return max; }}
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def lengthOfLIS(self, arr: list[int]) -> int: n = len(arr) dp = [1] * n for i in range(1, n): for j in range(i): if arr[i] > arr[j]: dp[i] = dp[i] + 1 max = 0 for i in range(n): if dp[i] > max: max = dp[i] return max
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(arr) { var n = arr.length; var dp = new Array(n); for (var i = 0; i < n; i++) { dp[i] = 1; } for (var i = 1; i < n; i++) { for (var j = 0; j < i; j++) { if (arr[i] > arr[j]) { dp[i] = dp[i] + 1; } } } var max = 0; for (var i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (dp[i] > max) { max = dp[i]; } } return max;};
